strengths: Strong SQL and database design skills
Proven ETL and data cleaning experience
weaknesses: Only 2+ years experience borderline for role
improvement_bullets: Optimized relational database queries to clean and reconcile complex datasets, ensuring high data quality for research and operational reporting, while documenting processes in Microsoft Word and Confluence to maintain standardized data management guidelines.

Collaborated with medical and operational stakeholders to facilitate data management, providing technical support for electronic record-keeping systems and training staff to enhance the accuracy of data collection.
Drafted technical reports and research documentation to support internal stakeholders, translating complex analytical findings into actionable manuscripts and summaries for departmental review.

Applied foundational knowledge of clinical research data management principles, including familiarity with electronic data capture tools and the capacity to rapidly adopt platforms like REDCap to support ongoing research studies.

Leveraged advanced SQL and analytical software to assist faculty and medical staff with custom query generation and statistical analysis, ensuring timely delivery of data management plans within high-pressure healthcare environments.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Analyst I-Emergency Medicine opportunity with Mount Sinai Health System. I am interested in this opportunity because of Mount Sinai’s global reputation for research innovation and its commitment to integrating artificial intelligence and advanced informatics into patient-centered care. What interests me about working for Mount Sinai is its focus on transforming complex healthcare challenges into actionable treatments; as a data professional, I find their mission to improve clinical outcomes through data-driven research deeply inspiring. I believe my technical proficiency in SQL and Python and my experience in managing complex data pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing data integrity and process automation. In fact, while working at Haven Technologies, I optimized relational database queries to clean and reconcile insurance testing data, improving data accuracy by 30%. This allowed our team to provide engineers with reliable data structures, significantly reducing project delivery delays. Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst I-Emergency Medicine role at Mount Sinai would allow me to bring my database management skills to ensure high-quality data reporting for the Emergency Medicine department. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my ability to translate complex performance data into clear, actionable insights. I streamlined client intake workflows using automation tools, saving 8 hours per week and allowing the team to focus on higher-value growth initiatives. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and statistical analysis to assist medical faculty and researchers in managing their clinical databases at Mount Sinai. I know that my technical expertise and my commitment to rigorous data quality align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Analyst I role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
